nasa_apod.title: Trifid Pillars and Jets
nasa_apod.explanation: Dust pillars are like interstellar mountains.  They survive because they are more dense than their surroundings, but they are slowly being  eroded away by a hostile environment.  Visible in the featured picture by the Hubble Space Telescope is the end of a huge gas and dust pillar in the Trifid Nebu...
